author: luca_zanolini
content:
Oh I see. So in gloas ‘the parent execution payload has been seen’ maps to bid.parent_block_hash's payload being in store.payloads ? 

But then,  if ‘parent execution payload has been seen’ means ‘in store.payloads’ (= verified), then wouldn’t the next clause (If verification is complete: [REJECT] parent passes validation) be dead code, since anything in store.payloads already passed verify_execution_payload_envelope? 🤔 

That clause only does work if a child can be accepted with the parent payload seen but not yet verified. So either seen != verified in gloas (and the on_block drop is reachable), or that REJECT clause should be removed. (Or I am not understanding something very obvious)

author: terencechain
content:
> So in gloas ‘the parent execution payload has been seen’ maps to bid.parent_block_hash's payload being in store.payloads ? 
yep

it reads like the following:
- if parent hash has not been seen -> queue it and drop it, dont penalize the peer
- if the parent has has been seen but was invalid -> drop it and penalize the peer
- else import it and forward it to your peer

author: terencechain
content: sorry maybe im not underestanding the question, let me know if that clarifies it

author: luca_zanolini
content:
The second bullet point, how is it possible? If seen = valid this would read

‘If the parent is valid but was invalid’ 🤔 

Im not sure if we are having troubles on terminology here…

author: terencechain
content: ah sorry im confusing you. Let me change `seen` from `verified` to `try to verify` so it's not like `i have seen it` it's like `i have verified it`, and i know the result of it. And the result may be applied to the subsequent checks

author: luca_zanolini
content: Got it now it's very clear: “seen” = “verified, verdict known” not ‘received the bytes’. Thanks for your patience! Would changing the wording from “seen/retrieved” to “verified” make it unambiguous, or do you think everyone already reads it that way and won't trip on it like I did?

author: terencechain
content: ya def. We are just used to reading it. The earlier p2p spec is now python code so it maybe less confusing to read. But wording change could be good. Cc <@592004585506340885>

author: luca_zanolini
content: Sounds good! I stepped into this due to a couple of proofs that only work if indeed seen=verified, otherwise we had issues. Happy to know this is already the case 🙏🏻

author: jtraglia
content: Because in this instance, I believe "seen" just means it was seen, not that it's valid. Terence is right that "this message has already been seen" usually always means a valid instance of it has been seen. Blocks are sort of a special case iirc. Here's phase0 spec:

author: luca_zanolini
content:
So, my main question here was

> When the gossip rule says the parent payload has been "seen", does that "seen" mean "verified" or just "received"?

author: terencechain
content: `seen` behaves more like i have verified it, and i have the result of the verification

author: terencechain

author: jtraglia
content: Which exact line in the spec are you referring to? It depends.

author: jtraglia
content: This one, right?

author: luca_zanolini
content:
consensus-specs/specs/gloas/p2p-interface.md, lines 287–293, the beacon_block topic:

- [IGNORE] The block's parent execution payload (defined by
  bid.parent_block_hash) has been seen (via gossip or non-gossip sources) (a
  client MAY queue blocks for processing once the parent payload is retrieved).
- If execution_payload verification of block's execution payload parent by an
  execution node is complete:
  - [REJECT] The block's execution payload parent (defined by
    bid.parent_block_hash) passes all validation.

My question is about the [IGNORE] on line 287–289: does “has been seen” there mean the parent payload has been verified (i.e., it's in store.payloads), or just received? It reads as “received” to me, and the separate [REJECT] is guarded by “if verification is complete”, which suggests a child can pass gossip before the parent payload is verified.

author: jtraglia
content: Yup that's how I understand it as well. That first condition is just received.

author: jtraglia
content: That's a bit of a mess imo.

author: terencechain
content: for prysm it's recieved and have the result of verification

author: terencechain
content: which is more or less the same

author: luca_zanolini
content:
If “seen” = just received, then:

1. A block B that declares its parent FULL can pass gossip while the parent payload is received but not yet verified, the [IGNORE] is satisfied by “received”, and the [REJECT] is skipped because verification isn't complete.
2. B is handed to on_block, which hits assert is_payload_verified(block.parent_root) (that needs the parent payload in store.payloads, not just received) , assert fails , B dropped.
3. Nothing re-runs on_block when the parent verifies a moment later, B stays dropped.

So an honest, timely B can be dropped at a node purely because its parent payload hadn't finished verifying yet. That node then attests to a different head, and if enough nodes are in that state at T_att, B loses weight and can be reorged, even though it's a valid honest block.

author: luca_zanolini
content: That’s the reason of this thread

author: jtraglia
content: Yes, I understand. This section of the spec needs improvements.

author: jtraglia
content: I believe the executable spec solves this. I don't believe it's possible to do that there. There's another condition in which it will ignore/queue the block if the parent state (including the parent execution payload) is not valid.

author: jtraglia
content: I need to say "valid" instead of "available" though.

author: luca_zanolini
content:
That [IGNORE] parent state available checks block.parent_root in store.block_states. So for a FULL-declaring B: B' is in store.block_states but store.payloads[B'.root] is still empty => IGNORE passes => reaches on_block => parent-FULL assert fails => dropped.

Or am I missing something? 

I think it has to gate on the parent payload, not just store.block_states. Then it's an IGNORE+ queue instead of the on_block drop,  which is the fix.

author: jtraglia
content: How would it reach on_block if it's ignored?

author: jtraglia
content:
> I think it has to gate on the parent payload, not just store.block_states. Then it's an IGNORE+ queue instead of the on_block drop,  which is the fix.
You might still be right. Something about this still feels wrong.

author: jtraglia
content: I'll need to dig more into it later though.

author: luca_zanolini
content: It’s not ignored. It passes, and the block proceeds. parent state is available, B' (the parent block) was processed, so B'.root is in store.block_states. So the predicate is satisfied => the block is not ignored on that basis => it continues to on_block.

author: luca_zanolini
content:
Would something like this make sense? 

/# [IGNORE] If the block builds on a full parent, that parent's execution
/# payload must be verified (MAY be queued until the parent payload is verified)
if is_parent_node_full(store, block) and not is_payload_verified(store, block.parent_root):
    raise GossipIgnore(“block builds on a full parent whose payload is not yet verified”)
